---
title: "Publications"

---

## Peer-reviewed articles

**\* = co-first author**

1. **Zhao A**, Cui E, Leroux A, Lindquist M, Crainiceanu C (2023). Evaluating the prediction performance of objective physical activity measures for incident Parkinson’s disease in the UK Biobank. *Journal of Neurology,* 270(12), 5913–5923. https://doi.org/10.1007/s00415-023-11939-0

2. **Zhao A**, Cui E, Leroux A, Zhou X, Muschelli J, Lindquist M, Crainiceanu C. Objective physical activity is the strongest modifiable predictor of incident Alzheimer’s disease in the UK Biobank *(submitted)*.

3. Cui E\*, **Zhao A\***, Crainiceanu C, Leroux A. Functional and Distributional Cox Models for UK Biobank Physical Activity Analysis *(in preparation)*.

4. **Zhao A**, Lindquist M, Caffo B. Comparing stochastic model performance with applications to fMRI data *(in preparation)*.

## Presentations

1. Sim S, Morelli K, **Zhao A**, Vogel J. L'effet de la phosphorylation de la tubuline-y sur la kinésine-5 et sur la formation de paires de microtubules dans le fuseau mitotique, *90e Congrès de l’ACFAS*, Université de Montréal, May 2023

2. **Zhao A**,  Cui E, Leroux A, Lindquist M, Crainiceanu C. Physical activity to predict Parkinson's disease in the UK Biobank, *8th Annual Joint Session of the BHS Lab, ENGAGE Lab, and SMART-WIT groups*, Johns Hopkins Univeristy, April 2023

3. Sim S, Morelli K, **Zhao A**, Vogel J. Le rôle de la phosphorylation de la tubuline-y dans l’organisation des microtubules du fuseau mitotique médiée par la kinésine-5, *89e Congrès de l’ACFAS*, Université de Montréal, May 2022

## Posters and Abstracts

1. Morelli K, Meier SM,  **Zhao A**, Willis M, Barral Y, Vogel J. Interrogating the material properties of a microtubule (+) end-associated nanoscale condensate in living cells using super-resolution microscopy, *American Society for Cell Biology Cell Bio Virtual*, 2023

2. Sim S,  **Zhao A**, Vogel J. Phosphoregulation of y-tubulin regulates anti-parallel microtubule bundling through kinesin-5, *8th Meeting of the International Union of Microbeam Analysis Societies*, 2023

3. Sim S,  **Zhao A**, Vogel J.  y-Tubulin phosphoregulation controls interpolar microtubule organization through kinesin-5, *Mitotic spindle: From living and synthetic systems to theory*, 2023

4. Sim S, Morelli K,  **Zhao A**, Vogel J. How does y-tubulin phosphoregulation control microtubule identity during spindle assembly?, *4th Annual CRBS Symposium*, Montreal, Canada, 2022

5. Sim S, Morelli K,  **Zhao A**, Vogel J. Phosphoregulation of y-Tubulin Directs Spindle Assembly Through Kinesin-5 and Microtubule Number, *American Society for Cell Biology Cell Bio Virtual*, 2021
